title: Reported cases of guinea worm disease
subtitle: Reported annual cases of [guinea worm disease](#dod:guinea-worm). Guinea worm disease is a painful and debilitating disease caused by a parasitic worm spread through contaminated water.
note: Due to limited surveillance capacity, particularly during past outbreaks, the number of reported cases can be lower than the actual number of infections.
caption:
Reported cases of guinea worm disease

Reported annual cases of guinea worm disease. Guinea worm disease is a painful and debilitating disease caused by a parasitic worm spread through contaminated water.

This choropleth world map displays reported annual cases of guinea worm disease for the year 2024. The legend uses a logarithmic scale ranging from "No reported cases" to "1 million," with specific thresholds marked at 10, 100, 1,000, 10,000, 100,000, and 1 million. The vast majority of countries across North America, South America, Europe, Asia, and Australia are shaded in the lightest blue tone, corresponding to the "No reported cases" category. In contrast, two countries in Africa—Chad and South Sudan—are highlighted in a light orange shade. Visually, these nations fall into the lowest non-zero band of the legend, situated between the 10 and 100 case thresholds. No countries appear in the darker orange or red bands that represent higher case counts in the hundreds, thousands, or millions. Several regions, including parts of North Africa and the Middle East, display a diagonal hatching pattern indicating "No data." A note accompanying the chart states that due to limited surveillance capacity, particularly during past outbreaks, the number of reported cases can be lower than the actual number of infections. The visual distribution shows the disease is currently reported in only a small number of specific locations, while the rest of the world reports zero cases or lacks data.
